title: TikTok's BookTok Community Launches Official Bestseller List
summary: In recent years, TikTok has become a powerful force in the book industry. The #BookTok community, with over 77 million posts, has transformed how readers discover and discuss books. Now, this influence is formalized with the first official UK BookTok bestseller list, compiled by Media Control and NielsenIQ BookData. The list combines UK sales data with analysis of the #BookTok hashtag, updating monthly. The inaugural top 20 is entirely female authors, with Irish writer Chloe Walsh appearing most frequently—all six books in her Boys of Tommen series made the list. The chart also highlights how BookTok revitalizes older titles; Donna Tartt's The Secret History, published in 1992, gained new popularity. Many listed books are upcoming screen adaptations, such as Colleen Hoover's It Ends With Us and Rebecca Yarros's Fourth Wing. The dominant genre is romantasy (romance and fantasy), with books by Sarah J Maas and Rebecca Yarros. BookTok influencers Kris and Mads note that romantasy is accessible and builds engaged fanbases. The list reflects that younger readers are more influenced by social media recommendations than traditional media. The influence of creators can cause immediate sales spikes, as seen when a self-published series sold out after their recommendation.

background_read: TikTok is a social media platform where users create and share short videos. The #BookTok community has grown rapidly, with millions of posts recommending books. This community has become a major influence on reading habits, especially among younger readers. The official BookTok bestseller list aims to capture this influence by combining sales data with social media trends. The list is dominated by female authors and the romantasy genre, reflecting the preferences of the BookTok audience.
Article_Structure: Main Points: TikTok's BookTok community has launched an official bestseller list in the UK, compiled from sales data and hashtag analysis. The list features all female authors, with Chloe Walsh leading. Older books like The Secret History have been revitalized. Romantasy is the dominant genre. Purpose: The article aims to inform readers about the formal recognition of TikTok's influence on book sales and reading habits. Evidence Evaluation: The article cites specific data (77 million posts, list compilation method) and quotes influencers, but lacks independent verification of sales impact. Author Credibility: The article is from BBC Entertainment, a reputable news source. Methodology: The list is compiled by Media Control and NielsenIQ BookData, reputable data providers. The article uses interviews and data analysis.

perspectives.description: They see the list as validation of their impact and note the accessibility of romantasy.
They benefit from increased visibility and sales driven by TikTok recommendations.
